_README: Geology-unit -> standardized lithology-class crosswalk consumed by src/data/rasterize_geology.py (vector stage) and, in numeric form, by src/features/hydrogeologic_domains.lithology_from_geology. Classes: 0 unconsolidated, 1 fractured_bedrock, 2 young_volcanic, 3 crbg.
_status: STARTER TEMPLATE — NOT an authoritative DNR table. The 'mapping' below covers the common controlled-vocabulary values of the WA DNR 1:100,000 Geologic Map 'LITHOLOGY' field, but you MUST verify and extend it against your actual extract: run `python -m src.data.rasterize_geology --geology <file> --list-units` and map every value that appears. Unmapped values become nodata (255), never silently misclassified. Have a geologist review the basalt age split before any hazard run.

refine._comment: Columbia River Basalt Group: Miocene basalt/flood basalt is class 3, not 2. Adjust 'column'/'contains' to your schema (e.g. a UNIT/FORMATION field matching 'columbia river' or 'wanapum'/'grande ronde'/'saddle mountains', or a GEOLOGIC_AGE of 'miocene').
